rules.rule: View services MUST transform foundation models to view models for UI consumption.
View services MUST inject foundation services, never brokers directly.
View services MUST NOT contain business logic (validation, business rules, authorization).
View services MUST handle UI-specific transformations (formatting, combining fields, calculating display values).
View services MUST map foundation service exceptions to view-layer exceptions.
View service methods MUST return view models, never foundation models.
View services MUST NOT modify foundation models.
View services MUST centralize UI transformation logic, not scatter it across components.
Date transformations MUST format dates for display (e.g., ISO string → "Jan 1, 2024").
Status mappings MUST convert codes to user-friendly labels.
Computed fields MUST be calculated in view service, not components.
ClassName generation MUST happen in view service for conditional styling.
Complex transformations SHOULD be split into private helper methods.
View services MUST be in `services/views/{domain}/` directory.
View service class names MUST follow pattern `{Domain}ViewService`.
View service interface names MUST follow pattern `I{Domain}ViewService`.
View services SHOULD expose interfaces for testing.
Each view service file MUST contain one service class only.
View services MUST NOT call other view services.
View services MUST handle null/undefined foundation data gracefully with default view values.
